lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Good to see you friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Martin. Now here's somewhere I wouldn't have expected to find you.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Indeed. I never thought I'd be back here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why are you here?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: We won! The Council are out, and we have what Kourend really needed. We have a king!

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Of course, King Artur ended up taking the throne from his father a little faster than anyone expected, so he needs plenty of guidance. That's where I come in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're helping to advise the King?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Exactly! When the people voted on Rose's Accord, they voted for the Hosidius family to become the new royal family. From a technical standpoint that means they voted for King Artur.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But in reality it was his father they expected to rule.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Precisely. My job is to advise King Artur and ensure his actions have a positive impact on the people that he now rules.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how's that going?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Not too bad. While he lacks experience, the King has a good heart. He wants to do the right thing, and he has the support needed to do it.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Mind you, he could do with listening to his mother less. She's always whispering things in his ear. She forgets that he's the one in charge, not her.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Still, I'm sure he just needs time to adjust. I have no doubt that under King Artur, Kourend will prosper like never before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure you're right. See you around Martin.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Until next time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's all this about?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Ah, hello and welcome. I'm Martin Holt, leader of Kourend's Royal Society. This is our little protest against the Kourend Council.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pleased to meet you. I'm [player name]. So, what's wrong with the Council?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Now there's a question! I have a better one. What's right with the Council?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I bet you didn't know that the Council was originally only temporary. After the last King died, they were only supposed to rule until his daughter came of age.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: However, according to the Council, the King's daughter went mad. Such a wonderful convenience for them of course. With her out of the picture, the Council became permanent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. What else don't you like about the Council?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Democracy! That's meant to be the big thing for the Council. Funny thing is, although they're meant to be elected by the people, the same four people have won every single election. Little odd, no?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That is a bit suspicious, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Indeed it is. So, do you want to join our protest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now. But thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Only a king can right the wrongs of the Council! And only you can make that happen!

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: How long will you keep your eyes closed? Their corruption surrounds you!

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: This kingdom will never be safe while the Council rule!

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: You deserve the truth! They won't give it to you!

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: The Council have wronged you! It's time to make things right!

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Together, we can bring an end to the treachery of the Council!

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: The Council care only for themselves! It's time for a ruler who cares for you!

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Kourend is a kingdom! A kingdom needs a king, not a council!

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Democracy? What democracy? It's all a façade!

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: People of Kourend, you have been lied to!
